title: Past Lives
logline: A Korean-Canadian-American playwright reconnects with her childhood sweetheart from Seoul after 20+ years, exploring the paths not taken while navigating her marriage to an American writer.
antagonistOrConflict: The tension between past and present selves, cultural identity, and the philosophical concept of In-Yun (fate/destiny)

actBreaks.description: Present-day framing device in East Village bar

validation.ruling: Scene #1 establishes the mysterious present-day situation with all three main characters, creating intrigue about their relationships. As a framing device, it effectively ends Act 1 by posing the central dramatic questions the story will explore.

actBreaks.description: Childhood in Seoul (late 90s) - establishes Na Young/Hae Sung connection and immigration

validation.ruling: Scene #2 is childhood backstory, not an act break. Scene #12 - Na Young's departure to Toronto on the plane - represents the true end of Act 1, completing the setup of childhood separation and launching the immigration story that defines the central conflict.

actBreaks.description: Present-day reunion - Hae Sung visits New York after 20 years

validation.ruling: Scene #47 shows Nora leaving NYC for Montauk after cutting contact with Hae Sung, representing her physical and emotional departure from their reconnection. This transition effectively marks the end of Act 2 and begins the final movement toward their eventual reunion.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Three distinct time periods: childhood Seoul, young adult reconnection via Skype, and present-day physical reunion. The script uses a circular structure, beginning and ending with the same East Village location, bookending the temporal journey.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Deliberately slow and contemplative pacing that mirrors the long stretches of time between connections. Extended sequences of Skype conversations (scenes 26-46) create intimacy through distance. The final act accelerates emotionally while maintaining measured rhythm.

incitingIncident.description: Hungover Hae Sung wakes up to water his mother left him. He remembers something, checks his phone notifications, and finds a Facebook message from 'Nora Moon' saying 'This is Na Young... Do you remember me?'
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #19 shows the catalyst event (drunk Hae Sung receiving Nora's Facebook message), but Scene #22 is the true inciting incident where Hae Sung wakes up hungover, remembers something important, and discovers Nora's message asking 'This is Na Young... Do you remember me?' This moment launches the main story by reconnecting the separated childhood friends and setting the central relationship conflict in motion.

midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑

validation.ruling: Scene #46 perfectly functions as the midpoint where Nora makes the pivotal decision to cut contact with Hae Sung, saying 'I want us to stop talking to each other for a while.' This fundamentally shifts the story's direction from reconnection to separation, representing the central turning point of their long-distance relationship and Nora's commitment to her American life.

climax.sceneHeading: INT. EAST VILLAGE BAR / SPEAKEASY ‑ LATE NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #98 serves as the climactic confrontation where all three main characters - Nora, Hae Sung, and Arthur - finally come together in the same space. This 10-page scene represents the emotional peak where unresolved tensions and feelings must be addressed face-to-face, making it the story's climactic moment of truth.

scenes.heading: INT. EAST VILLAGE BAR / SPEAKEASY ‑ NEW YORK ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: In an East Village speakeasy, Nora Moon sits with Hae Sung Jung and Arthur Zaturansky. Nora and Hae Sung talk intensely while Arthur scrolls his phone silently. Their conversation is muted, creating an enigmatic opening tableau.

scenes.heading: EXT. PYOUNGCHON ‑ SEOUL ‑ LATE‑90S ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: 1990s Seoul: 12-year-old Na Young (Nora) walks home crying after getting second place in exams. Young Hae Sung comforts her, establishing their childhood bond and his gentle, protective nature toward her.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ DAD'S OFFICE ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Na Young's parents pack their belongings while Leonard Cohen plays. The family prepares for immigration to Canada. Na Young and Si Young choose their Western names - Michelle and Mary - marking the beginning of their cultural transformation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Family decides to immigrate, setting the entire story in motion

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ MOM'S OFFICE ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: In her mother's art-filled office, Na Young's mother asks about her romantic interests while packing immigration documents. Na Young admits she likes Hae Sung, calling him 'manly.'

scenes.heading: INT. NA YOUNG AND HAE SUNG'S CLASSROOM ‑ ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Classroom scene showing Hae Sung and Na Young's easy intimacy as she draws flowers on his arm with a pen. Their relationship has a timeless, comfortable quality that suggests deep connection.

scenes.heading: INT. MOM'S OFFICE ‑ NA YOUNG'S FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Na Young tells her mother she will 'probably marry' Hae Sung because 'he's manly' and likes her. Her mother examines their new Korean passports while discussing this childhood romance with gentle amusement.

scenes.heading: EXT. GWACHEON NATIONAL MUSEUM OF MODERN CONTEMPORARY ART ‑
scenes.summary: At an art museum sculpture garden, Hae Sung and Na Young visit Jonathan Borofsky's 'Singing Man' sculpture while their mothers watch. The mothers discuss how well-suited the children are together, hinting at future possibilities.

scenes.heading: EXT. GWACHEON NATIONAL MUSEUM OF MODERN CONTEMPORARY ART ‑
scenes.summary: Later at the museum, Hae Sung and Na Young approach a granite sculpture of two faces gazing at each other. Hae Sung studies Na Young's face intently, a moment of quiet intimacy and longing.

scenes.heading: INT. NA YOUNG'S MOM'S CAR ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: In the car ride home, Na Young falls asleep with her head on Hae Sung's lap, their hands intertwined. Hae Sung watches her sleep, capturing the tender innocence of their young love.

scenes.heading: INT. NA YOUNG AND HAE SUNG'S CLASSROOM ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: On Na Young's last day of school before immigrating to Canada, she tells classmates she's never coming back. Hae Sung sits apart, trying to ignore the conversation but glancing over, struggling with the impending separation.

scenes.heading: EXT. NA YOUNG'S NEIGHBORHOOD ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung walks Na Young home for the final time, dribbling his basketball. He's cold and distant, unable to express his feelings. They arrive at her building and share a wordless, charged moment before she leaves.
scenes.turningPointNote: Final goodbye between childhood sweethearts, ending their first chapter together

scenes.heading: INT. PLANE TO TORONTO ‑ A FEW WEEKS LATER ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the plane to Toronto, the family practices English. Na Young and Si Young rehearse their new identities as 'Nora' and 'Michelle,' giggling as they repeat 'I'm fine. And you?' - embracing their cultural transformation.

scenes.heading: INT. TORONTO PEARSON AIRPORT ‑ CANADA ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: At Toronto Pearson Airport, the family goes through immigration with limited English. Nora sits on luggage while Michelle sleeps against her. Nora tenderly pets her sister's hair, showing protective instincts in their new country.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S TORONTO CLASS ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora's first day at a Canadian school, surrounded by mostly white students. She buzzes with nervousness, excitement, uncertainty, and loneliness. The bell rings, marking the beginning of her new life as '12 YEARS PASS' appears.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major time jump - Nora begins her new Canadian life

scenes.heading: INT. FIELDS ‑ KOREA ‑ LUNCHTIME
scenes.summary: Korean military service: hundreds of uniformed men march and eat together in fields. One anonymous soldier among them is Hae Sung, fulfilling his mandatory two-year service requirement, showing how his life continued in Korea.

scenes.heading: INT. NYC TAXI ‑ APPROACHING NYC ‑ DUSK
scenes.summary: 24-year-old Nora rides in a NYC taxi from LaGua dia to Manhattan at dusk. She stares at the gleaming city skyline in love with the view, now a mature woman pursuing her ambitions in America.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL ‑ CLASSROOM ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: In grad school, Nora receives feedback on her play from classmates and professor. She takes notes gracefully as they discuss her work, showing her integration into American intellectual life and artistic community.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: In her cramped dorm room, Nora and her mother google childhood friends from Seoul. They find Dong Yun became a lawyer and laugh about childhood memories, including how he helped cover up when she wet herself in class.

scenes.heading: INT. ALL‑NIGHT SOJU BAR ‑ SEOUL ‑ 3 A.M. KST
scenes.summary: At 3 AM in a Seoul soju bar, extremely drunk Hae Sung and friends console a heartbroken buddy. They philosophize about breakups and women. Hae Sung offers wisdom about new beginnings while his friend mourns his lost relationship.
scenes.turningPointNote: KEY MOMENT - Shows Hae Sung's emotional state and sets up his receptiveness to reconnecting with Na Young

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS OF SEOUL ‑ LATER, WEE HOURS OF THE MORNING
scenes.summary: Completely drunk, Hae Sung stumbles through Seoul streets alone as dawn breaks and the city awakens. Despite his intoxicated state, he manages to navigate home through familiar streets.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hae Sung quietly enters his family apartment using the automatic password lock, trying not to wake his parents. He maintains his childhood living situation, suggesting stagnation in his personal growth.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hungover Hae Sung wakes up to water his mother left him. He remembers something, checks his phone notifications, and finds a Facebook message from 'Nora Moon' saying 'This is Na Young... Do you remember me?'
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora reaches out to reconnect, launching the central relationship of the story

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S KITCHEN ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: In his family's ordinary kitchen, hungover Hae Sung joins his traditional parents for breakfast. His mother has prepared hangover soup. The scene emphasizes the routine, unchanged nature of his Korean family life.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL LIBRARY ‑ NEW YORK ‑ EVENING EST (MORNING
scenes.summary: At the grad school library, Nora receives Hae Sung's Facebook reply: a punny Korean response meaning 'Of course' (literally 'carrot') that reminds her of his childhood personality. She grins uncontrollably at his familiar humor.

scenes.heading: EXT. UPPER WEST SIDE STREETS ‑ NEW YORK ‑ MOMENTS LATER ‑
scenes.summary: Walking back to her dorm, Nora's excitement builds until she finds herself running through New York streets, unable to contain her joy at reconnecting with Hae Sung. Her physical movement reflects her emotional acceleration.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM/HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ NEW YORK/SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Nora bursts into her dorm and immediately opens Skype. Hae Sung calls her and they see each other for the first time in years. They giggle uncontrollably like children, marveling at meeting again and recognizing each other despite the years.
scenes.turningPointNote: First video call reunion - their adult relationship begins

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM/HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ NIGHT EST
scenes.summary: Hours later on their Skype call, they discuss how much they enjoy talking to each other. When Hae Sung must leave for class and Nora needs dinner, they reluctantly say goodbye, both wanting to continue their conversation.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ NIGHT EST (DAY KST) ‑ TWO
scenes.summary: Two hours later, Nora creates a Korean keyboard reference sheet and writes a long email to Hae Sung in broken Korean, beginning with 'I didn't know we would ever meet again...'

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SEOUL COFFEE SHOP/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung Skypes from a Seoul coffee shop while Nora, barely awake at 7 AM, answers from bed. Despite her usual sleeping habits, she woke up early just to accommodate his schedule, showing her commitment to their reconnection.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/SEOUL BUS ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora, dressed up for dinner, talks to Hae Sung who's on a bus to school. He discreetly shields his phone from other passengers as they discuss their days and her rehearsal schedule.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: At the end of his day and beginning of hers, Hae Sung teases Nora about her rusty Korean. She responds that she only speaks Korean with him and her mother, highlighting his special place in her linguistic and emotional world.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Nora tells Hae Sung she's shifted from wanting a Nobel Prize to pursuing a Pulitzer. Their conversation reveals how her ambitions have evolved while maintaining their grandiose nature from childhood.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung stares at Nora through the screen, telling her she's exactly like the 12-year-old in his memory - greedy, wanting everything, with a terrible temper. They laugh together, acknowledging her unchanged essential nature.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: As Nora prepares for class, they discuss her upcoming artist residency in Montauk. She explains its location by referencing 'Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind,' connecting their conversation to themes of memory and erasure.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑ LATE NIGHT KST
scenes.summary: Hae Sung watches 'Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind' alone in his room, following up on Nora's reference, showing how he's willing to engage with her cultural touchstones to maintain their connection.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL ‑ CLASSROOM ‑ MORNING EST
scenes.summary: In the library, Nora writes Hae Sung a long email instead of doing schoolwork. In voiceover, she asks when he'll visit New York, and he questions why he would go there, highlighting their geographical divide.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Nora asks why Hae Sung is going to China to learn Mandarin instead of coming to New York to learn English. He explains he's always wanted to learn Chinese for work, while she suggests her city as an alternative.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ EVENING EST (MORNING KST)
scenes.summary: Nora asks 'Can you hear me?' into Skype, indicating technical difficulties that will plague their long-distance communication and serve as metaphor for the barriers between them.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SEOUL COFFEE SHOP ‑ MORNING KST (EVENING EST)
scenes.summary: The connection breaks up as Hae Sung patiently waits for his screen to load, showing the frustrating technical limitations of their digital relationship across continents.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SEOUL ‑ EVENING KST (MORNING EST)
scenes.summary: From his room, Hae Sung asks 'Can you hear me?' into Skype, echoing Nora's earlier question and emphasizing their struggle to maintain clear communication across the distance.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL LIBRARY ‑ MORNING EST (EVENING KST)
scenes.summary: In the library, Nora impatiently waits for Hae Sung to load on her screen, saying 'Hello?' in Korean. Her impatience contrasts with his patience, showing different approaches to their connection challenges.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL COFFEE SHOP ‑ MORNING KST (EVENING EST)
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ MORNING EST (EVENING KST)
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. SEOUL CABLE CAR/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. HAE SUNG'S UNIVERSITY ‑ SEOUL ‑ AFTERNOON KST (LATE
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON JITNEY ‑ MONTAUK BOUND ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. MONTAUK RESIDENCY BARN HOUSE ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. MONTAUK RESIDENCY BARN HOUSE ‑ DOWNSTAIRS ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. MONTAUK RESIDENCY BARN HOUSE ‑ UPSTAIRS ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. BARN HOUSE ‑ NORA'S ROOM ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nora settles into her artist residency room at the barn house, adding her name 'Nora Moon' to the wall where previous residents have signed, and begins unpacking in the dusty but perfect space.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL SUBWAY TRAIN ‑ LATE NIGHT KST (AFTERNOON EST)
scenes.summary: In Seoul, Hae Sung writes a long email to Nora on a subway train but deletes it before getting off, unable to send his thoughts to her.

scenes.heading: INT. ALL‑NIGHT SOJU BAR ‑ SEOUL ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hae Sung meets his friends at a soju bar where they ask about his upcoming trip, and he downs multiple glasses of soju instead of answering when they ask if he'll be away all summer.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN HOUSE ‑ NORA'S ROOM ‑ AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: While Nora naps in her room, Arthur (the white man from the opening scene) arrives at the barn house by cab, though we only see glimpses of him from a distance and never his face clearly.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN HOUSE ‑ NORA'S BEDROOM TO KITCHEN ‑ MIDDAY
scenes.summary: Nora wakes from her nap, notices Arthur's belongings in the room across the hall, and goes downstairs to get coffee before heading outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN HOUSE PICNIC TABLE ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur meet for the first time outside the barn house, exchanging introductions and brief pleasantries, with Arthur acknowledging he got the worst room.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN HOUSE PICNIC TABLE ‑ NIGHT ‑ SOMETIME THAT SUMMER
scenes.summary: During a tipsy evening with other artists, Nora begins explaining the Korean concept of In-Yun (fate/providence in relationships) as her voiceover continues over scenes of Hae Sung.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of In-Yun concept that becomes central to the film's philosophy

scenes.heading: INT. PLANE TO SHANGHAI ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Hae Sung is on a flight to Shanghai as Nora's voiceover explains that In-Yun comes from Buddhism and reincarnation.

scenes.heading: INT. SHANGHAI DORM ROOM ‑ DUSK
scenes.summary: Hae Sung sleeps in his small Shanghai dorm room with a magnificent city view, surrounded by language exchange program materials, as Nora explains In-Yun between strangers.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHANGHAI NIGHT MARKET ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a Shanghai night market with fellow students, Hae Sung doesn't notice a cute Korean girl's interest in him while Nora's voiceover explains In-Yun's connection to past lives.

scenes.heading: INT. SHANGHAI NOODLE SHOP ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: In a noodle shop, Hae Sung makes eye contact with the cute Korean student and they smile at each other, showing mutual attraction, as Nora explains marriage requires 8,000 layers of In-Yun.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN HOUSE PICNIC TABLE ‑ NIGHT ‑ SOMETIME THAT SUMMER
scenes.summary: Back at Montauk, a tipsy Nora finishes explaining In-Yun to Arthur, suggesting they might have been connected in past lives, as other artists have wandered off, leaving them alone.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora and Arthur's romantic connection begins through the In-Yun concept

scenes.heading: INT. TORONTO PEARSON AIRPORT ‑ A FEW DAYS LATER ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur go through customs together at Toronto airport, with the officer questioning them about being writers and their visit to see Nora's family, revealing their relationship status.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur playfully make faces at each other across an East Village street while waiting for the light, then walk hand-in-hand as Nora reveals 'We're married.'
scenes.turningPointNote: Major revelation of Nora and Arthur's marriage, showing time jump

scenes.heading: INT. REHEARSAL ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Nora works with her director and stage manager in rehearsal, watching a Korean-American actress perform a monologue about crossing the Pacific Ocean and the costs of such crossings.

scenes.heading: INT. BOOKSTORE ‑ BROOKLYN ‑ LATER THAT DAY
scenes.summary: Arthur signs copies of his novel 'Boner' at a Brooklyn bookstore while Nora arrives with lunch, absorbed in her phone, giving him a quick kiss when she looks up.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APT ‑ LIVING ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: After sex, Nora and Arthur discuss food in a mix of English and Arthur's limited Korean, with Arthur using his one good Korean phrase before they decide on chicken wings.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ LATER ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Walking hand-in-hand, Arthur asks what Nora's thinking about, and she mentions Hae Sung's upcoming visit, explaining he's coming for vacation when Arthur asks why.

scenes.heading: INT. ALL‑NIGHT SOJU BAR ‑ SEOUL ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: An older, more mature Hae Sung drinks with his longtime friends at a soju bar, who tease him about going to New York to see 'that girl' - his first love who moved to America.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals Hae Sung's true motivation for the New York trip and friends' awareness of Nora

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITY ‑ WHEN HAE SUNG ARRIVES
scenes.summary: Hae Sung arrives in New York City to pouring rain, with the city showing its wettest and most unwelcoming face.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL LOBBY ‑ NEW YORK ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung struggles with his suitcase in the rain as rude New Yorkers bump into him, finally making it through the revolving door to his hotel lobby, completely soaked and squeaking with each step.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: In his hotel room, Hae Sung opens the curtains to see the relentless rain continuing to pour down on the city.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ AWNING AT THE ENTRANCE ‑ LATER THAT DAY
scenes.summary: Under the hotel awning, Hae Sung tries to light a cigarette while watching other hotel guests brave the weather with 'I ♥ NY' umbrellas, finally managing to light up.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ LOBBY ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: Having given up on sightseeing, Hae Sung lounges miserably in the hotel lobby watching local news, drinking and eating while the rain continues pouring outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITYSCAPES ‑ EVENING TO NIGHT
scenes.summary: Like a miracle, the rain finally stops across New York City as evening turns to night.

scenes.heading: EXT. MADISON SQUARE PARK ‑ NEXT DAY ‑ SUNRISE
scenes.summary: At sunrise in the quiet, post-rain Madison Square Park, Hae Sung waits nervously by the Reflecting Pool until Nora appears from the crowd, and they see each other for the first time in twenty years, both looking like they're seeing ghosts.
scenes.turningPointNote: The long-awaited reunion between Nora and Hae Sung after 20 years apart

scenes.heading: INT. MADISON SQUARE PARK SUBWAY STATION ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung walk down into Madison Square Park subway station, wait for the train, board it, and leave together.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY ‑ R TRAIN ‑ LATER
scenes.summary: On the crowded R train, Nora and Hae Sung stand holding the same subway pole without touching, awkwardly but sweetly smiling at each other over incomprehensible PA announcements.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK ‑ LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At Brooklyn Bridge Park, Nora mentions her husband for the first time, making Hae Sung wince, as she explains she emailed him during her Korea trip but he didn't respond, expressing her disappointment and desire to meet his girlfriend.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct confrontation of their current relationship statuses and past hurt

scenes.heading: EXT. BY THE WATER ‑ BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: By the water surrounded by couples on dates, Nora and Hae Sung discuss her marriage and his complicated relationship status, with Hae Sung asking about her dates with her husband and Nora explaining their East Village romance and fights.

scenes.heading: EXT. IN FRONT OF JANE'S CAROUSEL ‑ SOMETIME LATER ‑ SUNSET
scenes.summary: At sunset by Jane's Carousel, after a long silence, Nora asks why Hae Sung looked for her 12 years ago, and he explains he was angry she just left and wanted to see her one more time, leading to mutual apologies.
scenes.turningPointNote: Key emotional revelation about Hae Sung's motivations and their unresolved feelings

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora returns home to find Arthur playing Overwatch as a zen Buddhist robot character, and tells him he was right that Hae Sung came to see her, as Arthur's game ends in defeat.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hae Sung returns to his small, suffocating hotel room, takes off his shoes, sits on the bed, and stares out at the cityscape in profound loneliness.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ BATHROOM ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: In their bathroom, Nora tells Arthur about how Korean Hae Sung is and how it makes her feel both not-Korean and more Korean, leading Arthur to ask if Hae Sung is attractive.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ BEDROOM ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: In bed, Nora asks if Arthur is mad, and he admits he doesn't have a right to be since Hae Sung flew 13 hours to see her, jokingly asking if she'll run away with him to Seoul.
scenes.turningPointNote: Arthur's insecurity about the situation becomes explicit, raising the stakes

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Hae Sung gets out of the shower and into bed in his hotel room, turning off the lights to pitch darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ BEDROOM ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: In pitch darkness, Arthur and Nora lie in bed discussing how good a story Hae Sung and Nora would make as childhood sweethearts meant for each other, with Arthur casting himself as the evil white husband standing in the way of destiny, leading to deeper questions about their own relationship and what might have been.
scenes.turningPointNote: Arthur articulates the central dramatic tension and questions the foundation of his marriage

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Late at night, the romantically lit Dumbo pier shows Manhattan's bright lights with some remaining couples staying out all night and making out.

scenes.heading: EXT. LINE FOR THE FERRY / SECURITY ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung waits in the long tourist line for the Statue of Liberty ferry, beaming when he spots Nora approaching, and gives her a breakfast bagel he brought, watching her eat as they wait.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: On the ferry's top deck, Nora and Hae Sung lean against the railing in an intolerably romantic setting, with Nora unable to make eye contact while Hae Sung looks at her openly since he's not the married one.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora takes photos of Hae Sung with Manhattan and the Statue of Liberty, and when a tourist family offers to photograph them together, they politely decline, bewildering the family.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ BACK OF THE STATUE OF LIBERTY ‑ MOMENTS
scenes.summary: From the ferry, they see the Statue of Liberty from behind, with Hae Sung observing that she's turned against them.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ HEADING BACK TO MANHATTAN ‑ AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: On the return ferry, Nora shows Hae Sung wedding photos of her in a Korean hanbok with Arthur, explaining they married earlier than planned for her green card, as Hae Sung comments she looks young.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ LATER ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ NIGHT

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE RESTAURANT ‑ MOMENTS LATER

scenes.heading: EXT. NA YOUNG'S NEIGHBORHOOD ‑ PYOUNGCHON ‑ LATE‑90S ‑
scenes.summary: In a dreamlike return to their childhood neighborhood in Pyoungchon, 12-year-old Na Young and Hae Sung stand before her apartment building in the soft glow of late night, mirroring their first goodbye 24 years ago but now in a magical, timeless moment where they seem to have been waiting all these years.
scenes.turningPointNote: Magical realism sequence that bridges past and present, representing the eternal nature of their connection

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ 5 A.M. ‑ ALMOST DAWN
scenes.summary: On an empty East Village street at dawn, Hae Sung asks Nora what if this life is also a past life and wonders who they might be to each other in their next life. After saying 'See you then,' he leaves in an uber. Nora stands alone, then begins walking home, crying harder with each step.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final goodbye between Nora and Hae Sung, with the philosophical question about future lives providing closure while Nora's tears reveal the emotional cost

scenes.heading: INT. UBER ‑ DAWN
scenes.summary: Hae Sung sits alone in the uber's backseat as it drives away from Nora, mirroring their childhood scene in Na Young's mother's car. He looks out at New York waking up, feeling both massive and small as the strange city moves past him, and faintly smiles.
